Title: v0.2.8 — Generic importer + JSON cache + watcher reimport (Genericization Phase 3)
Summary: Third release of the genericization arc. The read half of the round-trip is built: a generic .tres importer mirroring v0.2.7's writer architecture (12 field-type read handlers, override registry, sub-resource recursion with _subId, AtlasTexture preservation). Manifest-discovered domains gain a JSON cache populated at boot reconcile + kept live by watcher reimport; the `/api/manifest-domain` endpoint serves values inline. Round-trip harness (parse → import → write → emit) locks the contract from both ends: karma 6/6 byte-identical end-to-end, faction 4/4, balloon 2/2 against the real FoB corpus. v0.2.8 = "manifest domains have a JSON cache, edits in Godot propagate to Bleepforge." FoB workflow unchanged.
Body:
## The headline

The **read half** of the round-trip is built. v0.2.7 shipped the writer half (generic .tres mapper, 12 field-type handler dispatch, override registry, karma byte-identical writeback 6/6); v0.2.8 mirrors the architecture on the read side and closes the loop with a round-trip harness.

**Karma round-trips byte-identical end-to-end through the generic path** — the same domain v0.2.7 used as the writer-half proof now flows through `parse → readFromManifest → writeFromManifest → emit` and matches the source bytes verbatim. Architecture locked.

**What's user-visible in v0.2.8**: manifest-discovered domains have a JSON cache. Open a Godot project whose `bleepforge_manifest.json` declares domains — boot reconcile walks every discovered `.tres` through the generic reader and writes per-entity JSON to `projects/<slug>/data/<domain>/...`. The watcher keeps the cache live: edit a `.tres` in Godot → watcher reimports through the generic path → JSON updates → SSE event fires → open Bleepforge windows refresh. The `/api/manifest-domain/:domain` endpoint serves entities with their values inline (single fetch gives the client everything for v0.2.9's edit form).

FoB workflow unchanged. FoB's seven hand-rolled importers stay registered as overrides per the (A) lock locked at the start of the cycle (editor-side overrides retire at v0.2.9 close, after FoB ports to BleepforgeRegistry).

## What landed in v0.2.8

### Generic .tres importer — the reader half

A 10-handler dispatch architecture mirroring v0.2.7's writer, covering all 12 manifest field types (string + multiline + flag collapse onto one handler; int + float share `numeric.ts`). Lives in `server/src/internal/import/generic/`.

- **Override registry** (`overrideRegistry.ts`) — `registerOverride(domain, fn)` / `getOverride(domain)`. FoB's seven importers register at module init via adapters that route cross-domain ref resolution through ProjectIndex (so the dispatch is uniform for single-file paths like watcher reimport).
- **Per-field-type handlers** (`handlers/`) — `string` (covers string + multiline + flag), `bool`, `enum` (Godot omits idx 0 → returns values[0]), `numeric` (int truncates), `ref` (resolves through `ctx.resolveRefByExtResource`; dangling refs stay verbatim as res:// strings), `texture` (ExtResource → absolute fs path; SubResource AtlasTexture → empty string so the writer's reconcile preserves it), `scene` (res:// path verbatim), `array` (recurses into orchestrator.readFlatFields for sub-resource entries; `_subId` populates from section id), `subresource` (single inline sub_resource recursing through the same flat-field walk).
- **Orchestrator** (`orchestrator.ts`) — `readFromManifest(parsed, entry, ctx)` walks `entry.fieldOrder`, evaluates `showWhen` against the accumulator-so-far, dispatches handlers, accumulates JSON. `array` + `subresource` get their own dispatchers (same pattern as the writer side).

**Validation:** 35 in-process smoke cases (`pnpm --filter @bleepforge/server smoke:import-{scalars,refs,arrays,subresource}`) covering scalars / refs / texture / scene / itemRef arrays / sub-resource arrays / single inline sub-resources / showWhen gating / dangling refs / AtlasTexture preservation. Plus the karma byte-identical reader harness (`harness:import-karma`, 6/6 against real FoB corpus — JSON equivalent to mapKarma's output via canonical key-sorted compare).

See [Generic .tres importer](/help/under-the-hood/generic-tres-importer) for the architecture.

### JSON cache + boot reconcile for manifest domains

- **Storage layer** (`server/src/lib/manifest/storage.ts`) — kind-aware on-disk layout. `domain` / `enumKeyed` → flat `<dataRoot>/<domain>/<id>.json`; `foldered` → `<dataRoot>/<domain>/<folder>/<basename>.json` (splits composite id). `discriminatedFamily` not yet supported (deferred — generic orchestrator refuses it too).
- **`.gitignore` markers** — `writeEntity` drops a `*\n` marker at each manifest-domain folder on first write. User-defined domain names can't be enumerated in the repo's root `.gitignore`, so the marker self-excludes the cache without churn-prone enumeration.
- **Boot reconcile extension** (`server/src/lib/reconcile/manifestReconcile.ts`) — runs after FoB's seven passes. Walks `manifestCache.listDomains()`, skips FoB-name collisions (defensive — projectIndex's classifier priority already wins), skips `discriminatedFamily`, runs `readFromManifest` per indexed entity, writes JSON via `writeEntity`. Results fold into the existing `ReconcileStatus` via a new `manifestDomains` Record.
- **Boot log one-liner** gains `manifest:` segments after the FoB section so anomalies pop without drowning healthy boots.
- **`/api/manifest-domain/:domain`** — now includes `values: Record<string, unknown> | null` per entity, read from the JSON cache. Single fetch gives the client everything for v0.2.9's edit form to consume.

End-to-end against `godot-lib/test-project/`: 11 entities written across 3 supported kinds (element × 4, note × 3, snippet × 4); equipment × 2 cleanly skipped with reason. JSON files at kind-correct paths.

### Watcher reimport for manifest domains

- **detectDomain** (`server/src/internal/tres/reimportOne.ts`) — returns a `DetectedDomain` discriminator: `{ kind: "fob" }` for the seven hardcoded mappers, `{ kind: "manifest" }` for everything else in projectIndex. `reimportOne` + `deleteJsonFor` early-return into a manifest branch when kind="manifest", keeping the FoB switch statements unchanged.
- **Per-file reimport** (`server/src/lib/manifest/reimport.ts`) — `reimportOneManifest(absPath, godotRoot)` mirrors the boot reconcile's per-entry loop but for a single file event. Builds the ReaderContext per-call (projectIndex-backed refs, canonical resPathToAbs, manifestCache sub-resources), runs readFromManifest, writes via writeEntity. Plus `deleteJsonForManifest` for unlink events.
- **Type widening** — `SyncEvent.domain` + `SaveEvent.domain` widened from the narrow `SyncDomain`/`SaveDomain` unions to `string`. The narrow unions stay as exhaustiveness anchors via `satisfies Record<SaveDomain, ...>` at definition sites; consumer lookups widen to `Record<string, ... | undefined>` so adding a SyncDomain literal still trips the type checker, but indexing with manifest names returns undefined cleanly.
- **Toast fallback** — manifest-domain sync events get a toast titled `<domain> updated externally` with the raw domain name and the entity key inline, routed to `/manifest/<domain>`. v0.2.9's edit page lands per-entity routes.

End-to-end: edited `welcome.tres` in test-project → bumped `CreatedAt` → watcher log `reimported note=welcome` → SSE event `{domain:"note",key:"welcome",action:"updated"}` → JSON cache updated.

### Round-trip harness — the contract gate

`pnpm --filter @bleepforge/server harness:round-trip [godot-root] [--domain=name]`. For each `.tres` in the configured FoB domain: parse (lossy) → readFromManifest → writeFromManifest (round-trip Doc) → emit → compare byte-identical. Both parsers feed off the same source text.

Results against the FoB corpus (59 files, 5 domains):

- **clean: 42 byte-identical** — karma 6/6, faction 4/4, balloon 2/2 (architectural proof), plus dialog 29/43 and quest 1/4.
- **limit-multiline: 14** — diff due to the lossy parser collapsing physical newlines inside quoted strings to spaces (`tresParser.ts:131`'s continuation-line `.trim()` + space-join). Writer also emits `\n` as escape sequences. Both need a two-spot fix for byte-identical multi-line string round-trip. Helper-layer concern, separate from the contract; tracked for a post-v0.2.8 cleanup.
- **differs: 3** — all quest files with stale FoB data (`TargetId` set on `Type=CollectItem` objectives where the manifest's `showWhen` excludes it). Writer correctly drops the field per the contract; `.tres` needs cleanup. **Surfaced as a feature**, not a bug — the harness is doing its job catching data hygiene issues against the manifest contract.
- **errors: 0**

**Karma 6/6 is the Phase 5 architectural gate.** v0.2.7's writer-half proof validated `cached JSON → writer → emit`; v0.2.8's reader-half proof validated `parse → reader → JSON ≡ mapKarma's JSON`. Phase 5 chains them: the reader produces the JSON, the writer emits it, the bytes match. Architecture locked.

## What's NOT in v0.2.8 (the honest list)

- **Edit support for manifest domains.** Generic `<DomainEdit>` + per-field-type form renderers + writeTresGeneric dispatch wiring all land in v0.2.9.
- **Production dispatch through the generic path.** Karma stays on its override registration through v0.2.8; v0.2.9 wires the dispatcher's fallthrough and retires FoB overrides at cycle close (after FoB ports to BleepforgeRegistry).
- **discriminatedFamily kind** in the generic importer. The orchestrator refuses these entries with a clear reason; the boot reconcile records them as `skipped`. Equipment in test-project + ItemData/QuestItemData in FoB are the affected shapes. Will land alongside v0.2.9's edit UI.
- **Multi-line string round-trip.** Lossy parser's space-join + writer's `\n`-escape emit prevent byte-identical round-trip for `.tres` with quoted strings spanning physical lines. Two-spot helper fix tracked separately.
- **FoB port.** Per the locked v0.2.6 decisions: FoB stays on hand-rolled autoloads through the genericization arc and ports at the end of v0.2.9.

## What's next (v0.2.9)

- **Generic `<DomainEdit>` component** driven by manifest field declarations.
- **Per-field-type form renderers** — text input / textarea / number / checkbox / select / AssetPicker / autocomplete-ref / array editor / inline subresource.
- **`overrideUi` mechanism** — bespoke FoB UIs (DialogGraph, NpcEdit, BalloonCard) plug in via the manifest's `overrideUi` field without losing the generic fallback for new domains.
- **`writeTresGeneric` wired through the dispatch fallthrough** — production saves for manifest domains flow through the generic path.
- **FoB port to BleepforgeRegistry at cycle close** — FoB's seven overrides retire, Bleepforge core ships zero FoB-specific code.
- **discriminatedFamily importer/edit support** — ItemData / QuestItemData / Equipment shape.

v0.2.9 = "Bleepforge edits any project whose Godot tree declares a manifest, including FoB."

> note: the deep dive lives in the **Genericization arc** section of the project's CLAUDE.md.
